italian: Mi senti? La connessione è lenta.
english: Can you hear me? The connection is slow.
choicesItalian: Mi senti? La connessione è lenta.
Mi vedi? Il PC è nuovo.
Dov'è il wifi? Non lo so.
choicesEnglish: Can you hear me? The connection is slow.
Can you see me? The PC is new.
Where is the wifi? I don't know.
correctAnswer: Mi senti? La connessione è lenta.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, si dice: Mi senti? La connessione è lenta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! You explain the problem clearly.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you say: Can you hear me? The connection is slow.

italian: Ti vedo a scatti, prova a riavviare.
english: You're lagging, try to restart.
choicesItalian: Ti vedo a scatti, prova a riavviare.
Accendi la luce, non vedo niente.
choicesEnglish: I hear you well, go ahead and speak.
Turn on the light, I can't see anything.
correctAnswer: Ti vedo a scatti, prova a riavviare.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la frase corretta è questa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great technical advice!
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the correct phrase is this.

italian: Sei in muto, non ti sentiamo.
english: You're on mute, we can't hear you.
choicesItalian: Sei in muto, non ti sentiamo.
choicesEnglish: You're on mute, we can't hear you.

Who are you? I don't know you.
correctAnswer: Sei in muto, non ti sentiamo.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, devi dire che è in muto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! A video call classic.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you need to say they are on mute.

english: Wait, I'm turning on the camera.
choicesEnglish: Wait, I'm turning on the camera.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Ti stai preparando al video.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're getting ready for the video.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, that's how you say it.

english: Can you share your screen?
choicesEnglish: Can you turn off the PC?

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo di condividere lo schermo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! To show a presentation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are asking to share the screen.

italian: Ti sento, ma non ti vedo.
english: I hear you, but I don't see you.
choicesItalian: Ti sento, ma non ti vedo.
Ti vedo, ma non ti sento.
Non ti sento e non ti vedo.

I see you, but I don't hear you.
I don't hear you and I don't see you.

feedback.incorrectItalian: No, la frase corretta è questa.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! There's only audio.

english: I'll send you the invitation via email.
choicesEnglish: I'll send you the invitation via email.

feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To organize the call.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, that's how you say it.

choicesItalian: Sento un eco, usa le cuffie.
choicesEnglish: I hear an echo, use headphones.

correctAnswer: Sento un eco, usa le cuffie.

feedback.correctItalian: Bravo! Un consiglio utile per l'audio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, suggerisci di usare le cuffie.
feedback.correctEnglish: Well done! A useful tip for the audio.

english: Sorry, I need to restart the computer.
choicesEnglish: Sorry, I need to restart the computer.
Sorry, I'm going to the bathroom.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You apologize for the interruption.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you need to say you're restarting.

choicesEnglish: There is too much background noise.

feedback.correctEnglish: Correct! You're signaling an audio disturbance.

choicesEnglish: I'm looking for the charger and I'll be right there.

feedback.correctEnglish: Right! You're solving a practical problem.

choicesEnglish: Maybe the connection will improve this way.
